title: Approaching Carnot Efficiency at Finite Power in an Experimentally Feasible Quantum Heat Engine
abstract: Whether a heat engine can approach Carnot efficiency while maintaining finite power is a fundamental question in finite-time thermodynamics. For classical Markovian heat engines with local interactions, the power-efficiency trade-off forbids an asymptotic approach to Carnot efficiency at finite power. In quantum systems, by contrast, degeneracy, symmetry, and collective jumps have been theoretically predicted to enable such an asymptotic attainment by enhancing activity. It has remained open, however, whether this mechanism can be realized in an experimentally implementable heat engine. In this Letter, we propose a superconducting-circuit heat engine that emulates the collective enhancement, thereby enabling an asymptotic approach to Carnot efficiency at finite power. This result demonstrates that, in an implementable model, such an enhanced dissipative mechanism circumvents the power-efficiency trade-off of classical Markovian engines. Our work connects abstract bounds in finite-time thermodynamics to a concrete circuit-QED platform and suggests a route toward quantum-device design based on collectively enhanced dissipative processes.

bite.hook: A new superconducting-circuit design bypasses classical limits to approach perfect Carnot efficiency at finite power.
tldr: Proposed a superconducting-circuit heat engine that emulates collective quantum enhancement.
Utilized collective dissipative processes to bypass the power-efficiency trade-off of classical Markovian engines.
Demonstrated that Carnot efficiency can be asymptotically approached while maintaining non-zero power output.
bite.whyItMatters: In classical physics, a heat engine can only reach maximum (Carnot) efficiency if it runs infinitely slowly, meaning it produces zero power. This quantum design breaks that rule, paving the way for highly efficient quantum technologies and practical quantum-device design.
bite.eli5: Normally, a car engine has to choose: go super fast (high power) or get amazing gas mileage (high efficiency). You can't have both. This quantum engine uses a special team-up effect among quantum particles to achieve near-perfect efficiency without slowing down to a crawl.

glossary.shortDef: The maximum theoretical efficiency that any heat engine can achieve under the laws of thermodynamics.
glossary.eli5Def: The absolute limit on how much heat energy an engine can turn into useful work, with zero waste.

glossary.shortDef: Describing a process where the future state depends only on the current state, not on its past history.
glossary.eli5Def: A system with no memory, where what happens next depends only on how things look right now.

glossary.shortDef: The study of light-matter interaction using superconducting qubits coupled to microwave resonators.
glossary.eli5Def: A technology where scientists use tiny superconducting chips to control quantum light and matter like Lego bricks.
